structural_claim: Quantum mechanics fundamentally alters the behavior of systems at extreme scales, creating new physics where classical descriptions fail

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Quantum degeneracy pressure creates a fundamental mass threshold where stellar structure transitions between classical and quantum-dominated regimes
The intersection of quantum mechanics and general relativity at event horizons creates fundamentally new physics absent in either theory alone
The quantization of the electromagnetic field resolves classical physics failures and reveals light's granular structure
Quantum correlations allow phenomena that surpass classical limits, creating fundamentally new states of the electromagnetic field
